title: All 5 'Doctor Who' Projects Still Confirmed After 2026 Christmas Special Cancellation

blocks.content: The news about Doctor Who's 2026 Christmas special being pulled, paired with the end of Russell T Davies' second spell as showrunner, has Whovians worried that the franchise is over. It's very important to bear in mind that there are still Doctor Who projects on the way. The show as a whole has not been canceled, and the franchise is actually set to expand. Without a clear way forward for the main show, I will admit that it feels like something of a consolation prize to have so much ancillary stuff going on in the world of Doctor Who. That being said, a lot of it sounds really cool.

I think what I find the most frustrating about the current state of Doctor Who is that the 2026 Christmas special was never actually going to happen. By Davies' own admission, he never wrote a script for the episode, nor were any actors approached to lead the cast. Despite rumors that the special had been shot with Billie Piper as the star, the former showrunner has passionately debunked those reports in a farewell post on his Instagram page. So, I can't help but feel deceived about the coming festive period. I was expecting a new Doctor Who adventure to enjoy with my family. Although that now won't be happening, there are still reasons to look to the future for my fellow fans of the long-running British sci-fi saga. Let's break them down.
David Tennant's Big Finish Tenth Doctor Audio Adventures

Once again, the soothing and reliable presence of David Tennant is set to carry Whovians through the current turbulence of the Doctor Who franchise. While the beloved Scottish actor has reprised his role for Big Finish before now, the upcoming set of Tenth Doctor adventures will be arriving at a very welcome time. Although stage and screen actors don't always flourish when thrust into a recording booth and told to recapture that same magic, I can tell you from experience that Tennant is still tremendous when he has only his voice to rely on.

Big Finish has confirmed that, after an initial 12-episode run as Ten in Summer 2027, another trilogy will land shortly after, in which Tennant's character teams up with other versions of the Doctor. A run-in with the Fourteenth Doctor would be mind-bendingly cool, but I won't hold my breath. Although Big Finish's Doctor Who projects aren't technically canon to the main universe made by the BBC, the audio adventures do tend to consider everything that's happened in live-action. As such, they make perfect sense and can generally just be assumed to be part of the official continuity.
Animated Kid-Focused 'Doctor Who' Series
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/movieweb-featured-image-template-89.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

In the press release from the BBC that announced that Davies was leaving (and that the Christmas special wasn't happening), it was made clear that another Doctor Who project would still be going ahead. "The previously announced animated new Doctor Who series for CBeebies is currently in production," the statement read. For US audiences, CBeebies is the BBC's channel for very young viewers, usually pre-school age. So, I wouldn't expect anything too cerebral from this project, which is being made in collaboration with Blue Zoo Animation Studio.

Despite being in active production, there's very little information about this series. It's not even known which Doctor(s) will feature. From what limited details have been shared, I would assume that it will take an anthological approach that doesn't focus too hard on any particular Doctor and/or companion(s). If it wasn't set to be aimed at an audience so young, I would say an animated Doctor Who project like this actually sounds pretty exciting. As it stands, it comes across as little more than a way to get kids started on Doctor Who at an early age. Maybe by the time they're old enough to appreciate the main show, it'll have its act together.
'The War Between the Land and the Sea' (US Release)

This 5-episode miniseries was the final release that spawned from the BBC's collaboration with Disney. For all of Doctor Who's missteps during the Disney era, I actually think this spin-off was almost perfect. It recaptured the dark, gritty tone of Torchwood, while fleshing out the canon of a classic franchise monster. It was released in the UK in December 2025, so I've already seen it. International audiences are still waiting for it to land on streaming. Given that it was made in tandem with Disney, I would expect it to appear on Disney+ when the spin-off's supposed 2026 release date comes around.

That being said, seeing how unceremoniously the deal ended between the BBC and Disney, it might be a little strange for the latter to suddenly host a new Doctor Who property months after things had fizzled out. I hope it does make its way overseas, though. It's criminally underrated. There are some game-changing twists in this macabre adventure that forever alter the Doctor Who canon, but not in a way that feels nonsensical. I'm looking at you, Timeless Child.
'Doctor Who: Circuit Breaker'
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/movieweb-featured-image-template-78.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

This one is right on the verge of beginning. On June 25, 2026, Jo Martin's Fugitive Doctor makes a sought-after return. Made in partnership with the BBC, Big Finish is helping lead the charge on a multimedia adventure that showcases the franchise's most famous pre-William Hartnell version of the Time Lord. As much as I dislike the Timeless Child storyline that introduced the Fugitive Doctor, I have to admit that Doctor Who: Circuit Breaker sounds really cool.

Whovians will be able to engage with audio adventures, comic books, novels, games, and even a bespoke in-universe UNIT website, and they'll all combine to tell one giant story. Big Finish has released a detailed release schedule of the various installments and when they'll land. Martin isn't just voicing the Doctor in the Circuit Breaker projects that require her to do so, but she's also written one of the novels that make up the collection. There's a risk I might give this saga a try.
'Doctor Who' Season 16
https://static0.moviewebimages.com/wordpress/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/movieweb-featured-image-template-90.jpg?q=49&fit=crop&w=825&dpr=2

Whether it's called Doctor Who Season 16, Season 3, or even another Season 1, the show is still coming back to continue the Doctor's story. It's the finer details that have yet to be confirmed. The press release that explained the 2026 Christmas special isn't happening made it clear that the franchise's flagship project isn't over, despite hysterical fans misinterpreting the news. What is clear is that the show will be very different when it eventually returns. There'll be a new Doctor, a new showrunner, and a new creative partner for the BBC to work with in the wake of Disney's exit.
I'm especially interested to see if/how Doctor Who's return will acknowledge that bizarre Billie Piper twist.

One of the most significant changes is that Bad Wolf Studios, which has been so central to Doctor Who's revival era, will play no part in the show's production going forward. Until the news about Davies and the Christmas special was announced, the BBC had confirmed that the next run of episodes would arrive around Easter in 2027. That hasn't been directly contradicted by the recent twists and turns, but I wouldn't be surprised if the BBC decided to stall while everything gets put in place for the show's next era. While that would be disappointing, I would much rather Doctor Who returns in a form that's as great as it can be, rather than being rushed back to screens for the sake of an arbitrary deadline.
Which of these are you looking forward to the most? Let us know in the comments!
